title: Unifolium amplexicaule

The plant is also known as Clappia amplexicaulis or Clappia amplexicaule in some sources. However, more recent classifications suggest that it may belong to the family Asteraceae. intro: Unifolium amplexicaule, also known as Riddell's indigo, is a perennial plant species native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may aid in wound healing and reduce pain. The bioactive constituents of Riddell's indigo include indole alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Riddell's indigo is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, infections, and pain management.
